Title: ZmMYB127 Modulates Maize Kernel Texture and Size by Integrating the Synthesis of Starch, Zein Proteins and Auxin

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: De18 promoter in transient reporter assays; Ubi promoter used for ZmMYB127 effector in co-expression assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in Results as a key endosperm-expressed IAA synthesis gene upregulated in zmmyb127 and downregulated in ZmMYB127-OE; treated as a focal downstream metabolic gene directly linked to the nutrient/hormone phenotype investigated.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a downstream metabolic node for modulating kernel size/endosperm development together with ZmMYB127-based engineering, but not the primary breeding target of this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in endosperm IAA synthesis as described by paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in endosperm; highly expressed in VE cells
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher IAA associated with larger kernels and more VE cells; downstream effects on storage distribution inferred
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in auxin biosynthesis leading to IAA production in maize endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates endosperm cell division, development and organ formation; influences kernel size
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher De18 expression is associated with increased IAA content and enhanced endosperm development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; promoter-LUC transient assay; IAA metabolite quantification in zmmyb127 and OE backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. De18 expression significantly increased in zmmyb127 kernels and decreased in ZmMYB127-OE kernels. 2. ZmMYB127 repressed De18 promoter activity in transient expression assays. 3. De18 upregulation was associated with increased endosperm IAA content and larger kernel/endosperm development.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TAR1 promoter in transient reporter assays; Ubi promoter used for ZmMYB127 effector in co-expression assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly named as Tryptophan Aminotransferase Related1 and described as a key IAA synthesis gene predominantly expressed in endosperm; directly assayed in Results.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: IAA precursor pool / indole-3-acetic acid pathway output
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node to tune endosperm auxin and kernel size in combination with upstream transcriptional regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in endosperm IAA synthesis as described by paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in endosperm; highly expressed in VE cells
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated IAA linked to larger kernels and altered storage distribution
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in tryptophan-dependent auxin biosynthesis contributing to IAA accumulation in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates endosperm development and kernel size
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher TAR1 expression is associated with elevated IAA and increased endosperm cell proliferation/development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; promoter-LUC transient assay; IAA metabolite quantification in zmmyb127 and OE backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TAR1 expression significantly increased in zmmyb127 and decreased in ZmMYB127-OE kernels. 2. ZmMYB127 repressed TAR1 promoter activity in transient assays. 3. TAR1 upregulation correlated with increased endosperm IAA content and larger kernels.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A decisive endosperm-specific regulator integrating starch, zein protein, and IAA synthesis to control vitreous endosperm formation and kernel size
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing/GWAS in 238 inbred lines; RNA-seq, NCBI PRJNA1172606; metabolite profiling of starch, protein and IAA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: endosperm, especially vitreous endosperm region of kernels
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms regulatory interactions with O2 and PBF1; likely acts in protein complexes/co-regulation modules on target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS, haplotype, CRISPR knockout and overexpression established ZmMYB127 as a positive regulator of vitreous endosperm formation. 2. ZmMYB127 directly represses starch synthesis genes Bt1 and Ae1, but activates major zein genes z1D2 and z1C1 through interaction with O2. 3. ZmMYB127 represses IAA synthesis genes De18 and TAR1, lowering endosperm IAA and reducing kernel size.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endosperm-specific; high from 9-27 DAP with peak at 12 DAP; enriched in VE cells during filling stage
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of starch and IAA synthesis genes; synergistic/additive co-regulation with partner TFs to activate zein genes or suppress starch genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Natural promoter haplotype variation; endosperm filling developmental program
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Starch and zeins determine kernel texture and quality; IAA controls endosperm development and kernel size
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Kernel filling stage; tested across three field environments for RVE GWAS (2019CZ, 2019XX, 2020CZ)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for marker-assisted selection and metabolic engineering to improve kernel texture, test weight and protein quality without obvious reduction in 100-kernel weight; requires caution because it reduces kernel size and starch while increasing vitreousness and zein
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor with two SANT domains
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Bt1; Ae1; z1D2; z1C1; De18; TAR1
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: KN5585; B104; B73 for expression assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype analysis; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; RNA in situ hybridization; Y1H; EMSA; transient promoter assays; Y2H; split-LUC; BiFC; GST pull-down; metabolite/protein quantification
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression decreases kernel size and starch content while increasing zein and test weight; knockout increases kernel size and starch but decreases vitreousness and zein
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endosperm-specific; preferential in VE cells; high during grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes vitreous endosperm formation and test weight, increases zein accumulation, represses starch accumulation, lowers IAA and reduces kernel size
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: storage reserve allocation and auxin biosynthesis in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter for overexpression/effector assays; 35S promoter for localization and transcription assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses Bt1, Ae1, De18 and TAR1; promotes z1D2 and z1C1, with stronger activation when co-expressed with O2 and stronger repression of Bt1/Ae1 when co-expressed with PBF1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural variation in promoter/5'UTR/exon SNPs alters expression and associates with vitreousness. ZmMYB127 itself lacks transactivation activity and functions as a transcriptional repressor in reporter assays, yet promotes zein gene expression via cooperation with O2.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important co-regulator enabling strong activation of major zein genes together with ZmMYB127
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (interaction detected in nucleus by BiFC)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physically interacts with ZmMYB127; previously known to interact with PBF1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O2 physically interacts with ZmMYB127 in yeast, Nicotiana leaves, maize protoplasts and pull-down assays. 2. Co-expression of O2 and ZmMYB127 strongly enhances z1D2 and z1C1 promoter activity. 3. O2 acts as a zein-promoting co-regulator in the ZmMYB127-centered network.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endosperm filling stage; expression pattern nearly identical to ZmMYB127
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation in cooperation with ZmMYB127
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage proteins influencing kernel texture, nutritional quality and vitreousness
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking partner with ZmMYB127 for enhancing zein accumulation and kernel vitreousness
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split-LUC; BiFC; GST pull-down; transient promoter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely promotes protein accumulation with potential allocation tradeoff against starch, as discussed in endosperm storage balance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar to ZmMYB127 during endosperm filling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances zein gene expression additively/synergistically with ZmMYB127
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: z1D2 and z1C1 promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates z1D2 and z1C1, with stronger effect together with ZmMYB127
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core co-regulator in Results because its interaction with ZmMYB127 was directly tested as part of the mechanistic model.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important co-regulator mediating stronger repression of core starch synthesis genes together with ZmMYB127
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (interaction detected in nucleus by BiFC)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physically interacts with ZmMYB127; discussed as part of storage reserve regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBF1 physically interacts with ZmMYB127 in multiple assays. 2. Co-expression of PBF1 and ZmMYB127 produces stronger repression of Bt1 and Ae1 promoter activity than either factor alone. 3. PBF1 is a starch-pathway co-regulator within the ZmMYB127 network controlling kernel texture.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression in cooperation with ZmMYB127
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Endosperm developmental program; normal nitrogen supply discussed from prior studies
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate shaping kernel texture and yield components
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering target for altering starch accumulation and texture, but manipulation may affect storage reserve balance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split-LUC; BiFC; GST pull-down; transient promoter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced repression of starch synthesis may reduce starch accumulation and alter kernel size/texture
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expression pattern similar to endosperm filling regulators
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strengthens repression of starch synthesis genes with ZmMYB127, favoring vitreous endosperm formation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: starch biosynthesis regulation / storage reserve allocation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Bt1 and Ae1 promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Additively represses Bt1 and Ae1 together with ZmMYB127
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core mechanistic co-regulator because the study experimentally demonstrates direct interaction with ZmMYB127 and functional promoter co-regulation in Results.
